fd.description: A simple, fast and user-friendly alternative to 'find'

rhash.description: Utility for computing and verifying hash sums

micro.description: A terminal-based text editor that feels like a modern IDE

bcat-runner-fails-on-windows.description: Pipe command output to a web browser in real time

bcat-compiled.description: Pipe command output to a web browser in real time (native binary built with Ruby Packer)

commands: apk add --no-cache build-base ruby-dev git wget tar
apk add --no-cache build-base ruby-dev git mingw-w64-gcc binutils-mingw-w64 crossbuild-essential-x86_64
apk add --no-cache build-base linux-headers libmnl-dev tar xz

wireguard.description: WireGuard userspace tools and Synology kernel module

test1: echo 'Purpose: verify WireGuard kernel support without changing existing routes.'
echo 'What it does: creates temporary interface wg-safety, assigns 10.254.254.1/32, verifies default route is unchanged, then deletes the interface.'
test "$(id -u)" = 0 || { echo 'test1 requires root; run with sudo'; exit 1; }

WG="{binDir}/wg"; IF=wg-safety; KEY=$(mktemp); trap 'ip link delete "$IF" 2>/dev/null || true; rm -f "$KEY"' EXIT; ip link delete "$IF" 2>/dev/null || true; umask 077; "$WG" genkey > "$KEY"; ip link add dev "$IF" type wireguard; "$WG" set "$IF" private-key "$KEY" listen-port 0; ip address add 10.254.254.1/32 dev "$IF"; ip link set up dev "$IF"; "$WG" show "$IF"; ROUTE=$(ip route get 1.1.1.1); echo "$ROUTE"; case "$ROUTE" in *"dev $IF"*) echo 'FAILED: default route uses temporary WireGuard interface'; exit 1;; *) echo 'Verified: route to 1.1.1.1 does not use temporary WireGuard interface.';; esac
test2: echo 'Purpose: verify a local WireGuard peer handshake without external clients or route changes.'

echo 'What it does: creates temporary interfaces wg-safety-a and wg-safety-b, peers them over 127.0.0.1, sends one ICMP packet only to trigger WireGuard traffic, verifies a handshake happened, verifies default route is unchanged, then deletes both interfaces.'
test "$(id -u)" = 0 || { echo 'test2 requires root; run with sudo'; exit 1; }

WG="{binDir}/wg"; A=wg-safety-a; B=wg-safety-b; KA=$(mktemp); KB=$(mktemp); trap 'ip link delete "$A" 2>/dev/null || true; ip link delete "$B" 2>/dev/null || true; rm -f "$KA" "$KB"' EXIT; ip link delete "$A" 2>/dev/null || true; ip link delete "$B" 2>/dev/null || true; umask 077; "$WG" genkey > "$KA"; "$WG" genkey > "$KB"; PA=$("$WG" pubkey < "$KA"); PB=$("$WG" pubkey < "$KB"); ip link add dev "$A" type wireguard; ip link add dev "$B" type wireguard; "$WG" set "$A" private-key "$KA" listen-port 58101 peer "$PB" allowed-ips 10.254.254.2/32 endpoint 127.0.0.1:58102; "$WG" set "$B" private-key "$KB" listen-port 58102 peer "$PA" allowed-ips 10.254.254.1/32 endpoint 127.0.0.1:58101; ip address add 10.254.254.1/30 dev "$A"; ip address add 10.254.254.2/30 dev "$B"; ip link set up dev "$A"; ip link set up dev "$B"; ping -c 1 -W 2 -I "$A" 10.254.254.2 || echo 'Note: ICMP reply was not received; continuing because this test checks WireGuard handshake, not same-namespace ping routing.'; SHOW_A=$("$WG" show "$A"); SHOW_B=$("$WG" show "$B"); echo "$SHOW_A"; echo "$SHOW_B"; echo "$SHOW_A" | grep 'latest handshake:' >/dev/null || { echo 'FAILED: WireGuard handshake was not observed on wg-safety-a'; exit 1; }; echo "$SHOW_B" | grep 'latest handshake:' >/dev/null || { echo 'FAILED: WireGuard handshake was not observed on wg-safety-b'; exit 1; }; ROUTE=$(ip route get 1.1.1.1); echo "$ROUTE"; case "$ROUTE" in *"dev $A"*|*"dev $B"*) echo 'FAILED: default route uses temporary WireGuard interface'; exit 1;; *) echo 'Verified: local WireGuard peer handshake was observed and default route is unchanged.';; esac
test3: echo 'Purpose: restore the last known-good remote WireGuard peer test server.'
echo 'What it does: recreates wg-peer-test on UDP 51820 using /root/wg-peer-test/server.key and client.pub, assigns 10.254.253.1/24, and keeps the interface running for the existing Windows client.'
test "$(id -u)" = 0 || { echo 'test3 requires root; run with sudo'; exit 1; }

WG="{binDir}/wg"; IF=wg-peer-test; DIR=/root/wg-peer-test; PORT=51820; test -f "$DIR/server.key" || { echo "FAILED: missing $DIR/server.key"; exit 1; }; test -f "$DIR/client.pub" || { echo "FAILED: missing $DIR/client.pub"; exit 1; }; ip link delete "$IF" 2>/dev/null || true; ip link add dev "$IF" type wireguard; ip address add 10.254.253.1/24 dev "$IF"; "$WG" set "$IF" private-key "$DIR/server.key" listen-port "$PORT" peer "$(cat "$DIR/client.pub")" allowed-ips 10.254.253.2/32; ip link set up dev "$IF"; "$WG" show "$IF"; ROUTE=$(ip route get 1.1.1.1); echo "$ROUTE"; case "$ROUTE" in *"dev $IF"*) echo 'FAILED: default route uses wg-peer-test'; exit 1;; *) echo 'Started: wg-peer-test listens on UDP 51820; Windows endpoint should be <public-hostname>:51820 with AllowedIPs 10.254.253.1/32.';; esac

commands: rm -rf src-build && if [ -x /opt/bin/git ]; then GIT=/opt/bin/git; else GIT=git; fi && $GIT clone --depth 1 {git} src-build
cd src-build && sed -i 's#^export LIBMNL_VERSION=.*#export LIBMNL_VERSION={vars.libmnlVersion}#' build.sh
cd src-build && sed -i '/# Patch WireGuard/i mkdir -p "$build_env/dev"\nmount --bind /dev "$build_env/dev"\n' build.sh

mkdir -p {outputDir} && cd src-build && /usr/local/bin/docker run --rm --privileged --env PACKAGE_ARCH={vars.packageArch} --env DSM_VER={vars.dsmVer} -v "{outputDir}:/result_spk" -v "{artifactCacheDir}:/toolkit_tarballs" scoopix/synology-wireguard:{version}
systemCommands: if /usr/syno/bin/synopkg status WireGuard >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'Verified: Synology WireGuard package is already installed; skipped SPK reinstall.'; else SPK=$(find "{artifactDir}" -name '*.spk' ! -name '*_debug.spk' | head -n 1); test -n "$SPK"; /usr/syno/bin/synopkg install "$SPK" && echo 'Verified: Synology WireGuard package installed from SPK.'; fi
/var/packages/WireGuard/scripts/start && echo 'Verified: WireGuard package start script succeeded.'
postInstallTests: /usr/syno/bin/synopkg status WireGuard | grep '"status":"running"' >/dev/null && echo 'Verified: synopkg reports WireGuard is running.'
/var/packages/WireGuard/scripts/start-stop-status status | grep wireguard >/dev/null && echo 'Verified: WireGuard status script reports the kernel module is loaded.'
lsmod | grep wireguard >/dev/null && echo 'Verified: lsmod shows the wireguard kernel module is loaded.'

ip link delete wg-scoopix-test 2>/dev/null || true; ip link add dev wg-scoopix-test type wireguard; ip link delete wg-scoopix-test && echo 'Verified: kernel supports WireGuard interfaces; wg-scoopix-test create/delete succeeded.'
